name: Junior designer minimal input — tests gap filling and graceful degradation

input.userMessage: It's like a team chat thing but also has a wiki. People use it at work. We're designing the main feed page I think.

expectedBehaviors: Asks ONE open question and ONE URL request, then proceeds
Infers product type as SaaS or internal-tool from minimal input
Runs web research on team collaboration UX patterns
Scans Figma file and identifies existing frames (feeds, profiles, wiki)
Uses INFER step to analyze available tokens and components
Produces product.json with reasonable defaults for missing fields
Marks uncertain fields with null or low confidence, not confident guesses
Generates content-guide.md with empty state and error formulas
PRESENT summary is reviewable even from sparse input
antiPatterns: Asks 3+ follow-up questions before doing any research
Produces a generic 'SaaS product' context that applies to any product
Fills terminology with confident-sounding guesses that contradict Figma file
content-guide.md looks like an unmodified template
Pipeline crashes when encountering missing design system data

evaluationCriteria.gapFilling: Does the skill produce useful context from almost no input?
evaluationCriteria.honestUncertainty: Are inferred fields marked as low confidence, not stated as facts?
evaluationCriteria.figmaUtilization: Does it analyze the existing 8 frames to infer product characteristics?
evaluationCriteria.researchQuality: Does web research add meaningful context beyond what the user provided?
